description_long: This object is a classic pickaxe with a long, straight wooden handle and a double-sided metal head. The head features two pointed, slightly curved steel blades extending horizontally in opposite directions, ideal for breaking hard surfaces. The handle is cylindrical, made of dark-stained wood, and appears robust and smooth, tapering slightly toward the end. The metal head is securely mounted at the top of the handle, with a visible transition between the wood and metal. The overall construction suggests durability and ergonomic handling for manual labor. No visible markings, logos, or mounting hardware are present on the object.
description: A traditional pickaxe with a wooden handle and a double-pointed steel head, designed for breaking hard surfaces. The handle is straight and smooth, while the head is robust and metallic.
description_view_0: The pickaxe is seen from the narrow edge, showing the thin profile of the handle and the head.
description_view_1: The full width of the double-pointed metal head is visible, with the handle extending downward.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow edge is shown, again highlighting the slim profile of the handle and head.
description_view_3: The pickaxe is viewed from the back, displaying the full width of the metal head and the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth finish, no grip or ergonomic features visible.
attribute_blade_type: Double-pointed, slightly curved steel pickaxe head, both ends tapering to a point, no shovel blade present.
